decision: Implement Option C strategy on instructions confirmed 23 May; long-stop notification deadline 14 August 2026 governs everything else.
rendered:
Decision: Option C strategy ready to execute on client sign-off 23 May. Notification deadline 14 August 2026 is the long-stop on everything.

What to read
- SPA clauses: 6.4 (completion accounts warranty), 8.2 (bring-down), 9.3 (de minimis £10k, basket £85k), 9.4 (£4m warranty cap), 9.5 (18-month notification standard), 9.10 (fraud carve-out), 11 (tax indemnity, £8.5m cap, 7-year notification to 14 Feb 2032), 14 (non-compete 24m UK / non-solicit 36m); Schedule 4 paragraphs 7.3 (customer warranty), 9.2 (change-of-control / no default), 12.1 (no tax enquiry within 12m); Schedule 7 paragraph 4 (earn-out one-off restructuring exclusion)
- Notification authority: RWE Nukem Ltd v AEA Technology plc [2005] EWHC 78 (Comm); Laminates Acquisition Co v BTR Australia Ltd [2003] EWHC 2540 (Comm) — non-compliant notification fatal
- Fraud pleading: Three Rivers DC v Bank of England (No 3) [2003] 2 AC 1 — balance-of-probabilities standard before pleading
- Restraint of trade: Tillman v Egon Zehnder Ltd [2019] UKSC 32; Mason v Provident Clothing & Supply Co Ltd [1913] AC 724 — vendor restraint reasonableness
- Privilege: Civil Aviation Authority v R (Jet2.com Ltd) [2020] EWCA Civ 35
- R&D tax: CTA 2009 ss.1052 and 1054 (qualifying R&D)

What to draft
- Three warranty notification letters under clause 9.5 (Belmont breach of Sch 4 ¶7.3; DLA misrepresentation / Sch 4 ¶6.4 breach; RouteCore breach of Sch 4 ¶9.2) — drafts by 6 June, served by 18 July
- Markel notification on RouteCore by 28 May (hard policy deadline)
- Preservation notice to Marsden & Bell (their files on TideSync) and internal preservation notice over Northshore IT data 1 Jan 2024 to date including Lockwood's consultancy mailbox (image first)
- Counsel instructions — Joshua Park KC (Fountain Court) on SPA / fraud; Sarah Mitchell (11 Stone Buildings) on non-compete injunction
- Atkins Bramwell instruction letter for FY2026 earn-out reconciliation (Schedule 7 ¶4 attribution question)
- Interim injunction papers for non-compete against Lockwood / ColdCircuit Ltd — application within 4 weeks of decision to proceed

What to chase
- Full Belmont Freight correspondence files from TideSync (incomplete on review)
- Complete RouteCore Systems correspondence to verify termination position
- Engineering scoping on TideMatch migration to firm up £320k cost estimate and 6-month timetable; impacts Markel IP/licensing sub-limit (£2m)
- Marsden & Bell's reconciliation working papers on the 11 Feb 2025 DLA journal entry
- Lockwood personal asset position — security under SPA presently only nominal (ColdCircuit shareholding)
- Intelligence on ColdCircuit Ltd dealings with Aldwell Distribution and Norchill Foods — both former TideSync clients
- HMRC appeal status on £180k disallowed credits — Marsden & Bell handling
- Eversfield Quinn formal conflict confirmation (received 28 Apr 2026 — confirm on file)
- Counsel availability for 27 June conference

claims.claim: Option C is the recommended strategy; client sign-off due 23 May 2026
Long-stop notification deadline 14 August 2026
SPA clause 9.5 notification standard requires written notice within 18 months with reasonable detail of the matter, breach and amount
Warranty cap £4m (clause 9.4) with de minimis £10k and basket £85k (clause 9.3)
Tax indemnity separately capped at £8.5m with 7-year notification window to 14 February 2032
Fraud carve-out at clause 9.10 removes cap and exclusion protection
Authority on notification non-compliance being fatal: RWE Nukem and Laminates Acquisition
Fraud pleading standard: Three Rivers DC v Bank of England (No 3)
Restraint of trade reasonableness authorities: Tillman v Egon Zehnder and Mason v Provident
Litigation privilege: CAA v Jet2.com
Markel RouteCore notification due 28 May 2026
Notification drafts by 6 June, served 18 July, long-stop 14 August
Counsel: Joshua Park KC (Fountain Court) for SPA/fraud; Sarah Mitchell (11 Stone Buildings) for non-compete injunction
Schedule 7 paragraph 4 excludes one-off restructuring costs from earn-out accounts — governs the FY2026 reconciliation question
Lockwood's personal asset position uncertain; SPA security over ColdCircuit shareholding nominal
Marsden & Bell potential professional negligence exposure on the DLA reclassification — hold in reserve
Image Lockwood's consultancy mailbox before further action
Markel W&I IP/licensing sub-limit £2m bears on RouteCore quantum sizing
Counsel conference scheduled 27 June 2026
Eversfield Quinn conflicted out of dispute work — confirmation 28 April 2026
